candidates.evidence_quote: Comparable restaurant sales at The Cheesecake Factory restaurants declined 0.6% year-over-year in the first quarter of fiscal 2024.
North Italia operating information: Comparable restaurant sales vs. prior year 3 %
The Cheesecake Factory restaurants comparable sales and traffic once again meaningfully outperformed the industry
Our operators continued to deliver improvements in labor productivity, food efficiency, and hourly staff and manager retention, all of which contributed to significant growth in profitability.
improvements in labor productivity, food efficiency, and hourly staff and manager retention
Food and beverage costs 203,253 22.8 % 206,224 23.8 %
The Company continues to expect to open as many as 22 new restaurants in fiscal 2024
Preopening costs 5,880 0.6 % 3,052 0.3 %
Impairment of assets and lease termination expenses 2,083 0.2 % 2,242 0.3 %
The Company recorded a pre-tax net expense of $3.2 million related to impairment of assets and lease termination expenses
Acquisition-related contingent consideration, compensation and amortization expenses
The Company repurchased approximately 359,400 shares of its stock at a cost of $12.5 million in the first quarter of fiscal 2024.
Board of Directors has declared a quarterly dividend of $0.27 per share to be paid on June 4, 2024
Our first quarter sales improved throughout the quarter culminating at the high-end of our expectations.
Net income and diluted net income per share were $33.2 million and $0.68, respectively, in the first quarter of fiscal 2024.
Income from operations 39,293 4.4 % 33,926 3.9 %
General and administrative expenses 60,366 6.8 % 54,069 6.2 %

notes: Chunk contains a single event: the Q1 FY2024 earnings 8-K press release (accession 0001104659-24-058691); no fiscal_kpis array in this chunk.

Flagship-brand comparable sales named same_store_sales because the brand name equals the company legal name (banned in driver_name); North Italia is a distinct brand so it keeps its own brand-level driver.

Macro/narrative candidates (interest_rates, inflation, tariffs, FX, minimum_wage, unemployment_rate, weather, supply chain, consumer confidence/spending) come from the safe-harbor factors paragraph - boilerplate but real source text naming specific causal variables.

Skipped as vague/non-causal/instance-bound (14): pandemic dining restrictions, demonstrations/political unrest, armed conflicts, landlord/tenant economic health, debt covenant compliance, uncertain tax positions with IRS, international market acceptance (contains company name), total available liquidity/revolver availability, convertible notes/debt balance, depreciation and amortization line, interest and other expense line, segment revenue levels (outcomes covered by same_store_sales + new_unit_development), permitting timing, Fortune 100 Best Companies listing.
FRC (Fox Restaurant Concepts) segment metrics not coined as drivers because FRC is an acquired company legal name (banned); its acquisition cost impact is captured under acquisition_related_costs.
